title: Pseudoraphis squarrosa
taxonomy: Pseudoraphis squarrosa is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The species belongs to the genus Pseudoraphis, which consists of a few species of grasses. Pseudoraphis squarrosa is further classified within the tribe Paniceae, a group of grasses commonly found in warm and temperate regions. This classification helps to identify and understand the evolutionary relationships between different species of grasses.
uses: Pseudoraphis squarrosa is a type of grass found in tropical and subtropical regions, but limited information is available on its specific uses in traditional medicine.
